explanation_box.text: It's a small active reptile that lives in the trees where its eggs are safe.
The red color makes mates attracted to it.
The red color also makes the young easy prey on the jungle floor.
This reptile is well-camoflaged and produces a lot of eggs.
Red attracts mates but is a disadvantage once the young are born.
The yellowish-green color gives it decent camoflage.
Red is a color that attracts predators, and the sharp claws don't help it run fast.
The rounded feet make it more adapted for escape and the red color attracts mates.
Another red one that is moderately successful.
Green is a good color for blending in with the foliage, but the sharp toes are not optimal.
This one has the green color, which is good, and the rounded toes that make it fast.
Another one that is successful at escaping with its rounded toes.
This one has the sharp toes and a red color which is a bad combination.
The sharp toes are not a good adaptation, and a lot of the offspring end up getting eaten by predators.

prompt_continuous: Reptiles varied in whether they had ROUND feet or POINTY, CURLY feet. Did having CURLY/POINTY or ROUND feet make a difference to fertility? If you did not notice this feature or believe it did not make a difference to fertility, please hit no and proceed.
question_text: Did foot shape matter to the fertility of reptiles?
